sections.subtitle: Current Form
sections.text: Deportivo Alavés have had a mixed run in their last 5 matches, with 2 wins, 1 draw, and 2 losses, showing inconsistency but a strong performance in their 7-0 cup win against CD Getxo. Espanyol, on the other hand, are in better form with 3 wins in their last 5 matches, including a solid 2-0 victory over Real Oviedo, indicating momentum going into this clash.

sections.subtitle: Head-to-Head Record
sections.text: In their last two head-to-head encounters, Espanyol have dominated with wins in both matches (3-2 and 1-0), scoring late in critical moments. Alavés have struggled to keep clean sheets against Espanyol, conceding in their last 5 meetings, which could be a concern for today's game.

sections.subtitle: Season Statistics
sections.text: Alavés, sitting 12th with 12 points, have a balanced record with 9 goals scored and conceded, excelling defensively (least goals conceded rank 1) but lacking in attack (18th in goals scored). Espanyol, positioned 5th with 18 points, have a stronger offensive output (14 goals) and are on a 3-match winning streak, making them slight favorites.

sections.subtitle: Key Players to Watch
sections.text: For Alavés, Carlos Vicente and Mariano Díaz are in form, with Vicente contributing 3 goals and 2 assists in recent games. Espanyol will rely on their attacking structure despite missing key defender Carlos Romero, with players like Roberto Fernández creating big chances.

sections.subtitle: Tactical Insights
sections.text: Alavés have shown strength in tackles (1st in league) and corners (3rd per game), which could help them control set pieces. Espanyol’s ability to score early (first to score in 4/5 recent games) and their efficiency in big chances may test Alavés’ defensive resilience.

sections.subtitle: Match Prediction
sections.text: Given Espanyol’s recent form, head-to-head advantage, and higher league standing, they edge out as favorites, though Sofascore’s model suggests a high likelihood of a draw (39%). Alavés’ home advantage at Estadio de Mendizorroza and defensive solidity could keep it tight, but Espanyol’s momentum might secure a narrow victory.

halftimeSections.subtitle: First half overview
halftimeSections.text: The first half at Estadio de Mendizorroza saw Deportivo Alavés take a commanding lead over Espanyol with a 2-0 scoreline. Alavés struck early through Denis Suárez and added a second goal late in the half via Lucas Boyé, showcasing their clinical finishing. The home side dominated possession and created more dangerous opportunities, while Espanyol struggled to make an impact in the final third despite some attempts to push forward. Alavés displayed greater control and composure, setting a strong foundation for the second half.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Home team performance
halftimeSections.text: Deportivo Alavés put in a solid performance in the opening 45 minutes. Their dominance in possession translated into frequent entries into the final third and penalty area, creating multiple scoring chances. Key players like Denis Suárez and Lucas Boyé were instrumental in breaking through Espanyol’s defense, with both finding the back of the net. The midfield, anchored by players like Antonio Blanco, provided stability, winning duels and maintaining pressure. Defensively, Alavés looked organized, limiting Espanyol to minimal clear-cut opportunities.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Away team performance
halftimeSections.text: Espanyol had a challenging first half, failing to match Alavés’ intensity and creativity. They struggled to retain possession and create meaningful chances, often finding themselves on the back foot. Despite a few entries into the penalty area, their attacking efforts lacked precision, with only a handful of shots troubling the Alavés defense. Defensively, they were exposed at key moments, unable to prevent the two goals. Espanyol will need to regroup and show more urgency if they hope to turn things around in the second half.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Attack momentum analysis
halftimeSections.text: The first half saw distinct shifts in attacking momentum, with Alavés largely dictating the pace. From the opening whistle, the home team launched several threatening spells, particularly in the early minutes, which led to their first goal. They maintained consistent pressure with waves of attacks through the middle of the half, often pinning Espanyol back. The away side had brief moments of forward push, especially around the midway point, but couldn’t sustain their momentum. Towards the end of the half, Alavés regained control, culminating in their second goal with another strong attacking surge.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Second half outlook
halftimeSections.text: Looking ahead to the second half, Deportivo Alavés are in a strong position to maintain their lead. Their attacking intent and control of the game suggest they could add to their tally if they continue to exploit spaces in Espanyol’s defense. Espanyol, on the other hand, will likely come out with renewed energy, desperate to reduce the deficit. They may adopt a more aggressive approach, taking risks to create chances. However, Alavés’ defensive organization and ability to counter could pose significant problems for the visitors.
